name: Strategic Marigold Planting
description: Plant around the tomato plants to confuse pests who are trying to find the plants.
instructions: French marigolds are the best. Plant them BEFORE you plant your tomatoes for this to work.

name: Lasagna-Style Composting
description: Alternating layers of materials to make quick composted soil without turning the pile.
materials: 1-part brown material (cardboard, paper, newspaper -- nothing glossy)
instructions: Layer your different materials -- like you would make a dish of lasagna. You can add as much or as little to your pile and keep piling it up. The heat from the pile will compost without you turning the pile. Make sure to have it in a shaded area and keep it moist, but not sopping wet. You can use the composted soil in about 3-4 months.

name: Natural Insect Deterrents
description: Diatomaceous Earth is great for pests, especially slugs and ants. The powder has razor-blade-like molecules that cause tiny cuts on the soft-bellied insects.
instructions: Make a ring around the base of the plant to keep ground insects at bay. Reapply after rain.
